algorithm: My algorithm uses an aggressive mutation and crowding-distance-based selection with differential evolution to maintain diversity, combined with a reference-point-based hypervolume contribution archive update.
code:
import numpy as np

class Metaheuristic:

    def __init__(self, func, dim, bounds, budget, n_obj):
        self.func   = func
        self.dim    = dim
        self.bounds = bounds
        self.budget = budget
        self.n_obj  = n_obj

    def solve(self):
        lo, hi = self.bounds[0], self.bounds[1]
        pop_size = 50
        max_gen = (self.budget - pop_size) // pop_size
        evals = 0

        # Initialize population
        pop_x = np.random.uniform(lo, hi, (pop_size, self.dim))
        pop_f = np.array([self.func(x) for x in pop_x])
        evals += pop_size

        # Archive
        archive_x = list(pop_x)
        archive_f = list(pop_f)

        for gen in range(max_gen):
            # Differential evolution mutation and crossover
            offspring_x = np.empty_like(pop_x)
            for i in range(pop_size):
                idxs = np.random.choice(pop_size, 3, replace=False)
                a, b, c = pop_x[idxs]
                # DE/rand/1 with aggressive F
                F = 0.9 + 0.1 * np.random.rand()
                mutant = a + F * (b - c)
                # Crossover with higher CR
                CR = 0.9
                child = pop_x[i].copy()
                j_rand = np.random.randint(self.dim)
                for j in range(self.dim):
                    if np.random.rand() < CR or j == j_rand:
                        child[j] = mutant[j]
                child = np.clip(child, lo, hi)
                offspring_x[i] = child

            offspring_f = np.array([self.func(x) for x in offspring_x])
            evals += pop_size

            # Non-dominated sorting + crowding distance selection
            combined_x = np.vstack([pop_x, offspring_x])
            combined_f = np.vstack([pop_f, offspring_f])
            n_comb = len(combined_f)

            # Compute domination and crowding distance
            fronts = []
            remaining = np.ones(n_comb, dtype=bool)
            while remaining.any():
                idx_rem = np.where(remaining)[0]
                f_rem = combined_f[idx_rem]
                nd = np.ones(len(idx_rem), dtype=bool)
                for i in range(len(idx_rem)):
                    dominated_by = np.all(f_rem <= f_rem[i], axis=1) & np.any(f_rem < f_rem[i], axis=1)
                    if dominated_by.sum() > 1:  # more than itself
                        nd[i] = False
                fronts.append(idx_rem[nd])
                remaining[idx_rem[nd]] = False

            # Select pop_size individuals
            selected = []
            for front in fronts:
                if len(selected) + len(front) <= pop_size:
                    selected.extend(front)
                else:
                    # Crowding distance on this front
                    f_front = combined_f[front]
                    cd = np.zeros(len(front))
                    for obj in range(self.n_obj):
                        order = np.argsort(f_front[:, obj])
                        cd[order[0]] = np.inf
                        cd[order[-1]] = np.inf
                        for k in range(1, len(order)-1):
                            cd[order[k]] += (f_front[order[k+1], obj] - f_front[order[k-1], obj])
                    # Select by crowding distance descending
                    remain = pop_size - len(selected)
                    if remain > 0:
                        top_idx = np.argsort(-cd)[:remain]
                        selected.extend(front[top_idx])
                    break

            pop_x = combined_x[selected]
            pop_f = combined_f[selected]

            # Update archive
            for i in range(pop_size):
                archive_x.append(pop_x[i].copy())
                archive_f.append(pop_f[i].copy())

        # Final non-dominated filtering
        X = np.array(archive_x)
        F = np.array(archive_f)
        nd = np.ones(len(F), dtype=bool)
        for i in range(len(F)):
            dominated_by = np.all(F <= F[i], axis=1) & np.any(F < F[i], axis=1)
            dominated_by[i] = False
            if dominated_by.any():
                nd[i] = False
        return X[nd]

algorithm: The new algorithm uses a differential evolution (DE) based approach with non-dominated sorting and crowding distance selection to iteratively evolve a population towards the Pareto front.
code:
import numpy as np

class Metaheuristic:

    def __init__(self, func, dim, bounds, budget, n_obj):
        self.func   = func
        self.dim    = dim
        self.bounds = bounds
        self.budget = budget
        self.n_obj  = n_obj

    def solve(self):
        lo, hi = self.bounds[0], self.bounds[1]
        pop_size = 50
        generations = self.budget // pop_size
        
        # Initialize population
        pop = lo + (hi - lo) * np.random.rand(pop_size, self.dim)
        pop_f = np.array([self.func(p) for p in pop])
        evals = pop_size
        
        for gen in range(generations - 1):
            if evals >= self.budget:
                break
            
            # Create offspring via DE mutation and crossover
            offspring = np.zeros_like(pop)
            for i in range(pop_size):
                idxs = [idx for idx in range(pop_size) if idx != i]
                a, b, c = pop[np.random.choice(idxs, 3, replace=False)]
                mutant = a + 0.5 * (b - c)
                # Clip to bounds
                mutant = np.clip(mutant, lo, hi)
                # Crossover
                cross_prob = 0.9
                j_rand = np.random.randint(self.dim)
                trial = np.array([mutant[j] if np.random.rand() < cross_prob or j == j_rand else pop[i, j] for j in range(self.dim)])
                offspring[i] = trial
            
            offspring_f = np.array([self.func(o) for o in offspring])
            evals += pop_size
            
            # Combine parent and offspring
            combined_pop = np.vstack([pop, offspring])
            combined_f = np.vstack([pop_f, offspring_f])
            
            # Non-dominated sorting with crowding distance
            n = len(combined_pop)
            dominance_count = np.zeros(n, dtype=int)
            dominated_set = [[] for _ in range(n)]
            fronts = [[]]
            
            for p in range(n):
                for q in range(n):
                    if np.all(combined_f[p] <= combined_f[q]) and np.any(combined_f[p] < combined_f[q]):
                        dominated_set[p].append(q)
                    elif np.all(combined_f[q] <= combined_f[p]) and np.any(combined_f[q] < combined_f[p]):
                        dominance_count[p] += 1
                if dominance_count[p] == 0:
                    fronts[0].append(p)
            
            front_idx = 0
            front_list = []
            while fronts[front_idx]:
                next_front = []
                for p in fronts[front_idx]:
                    for q in dominated_set[p]:
                        dominance_count[q] -= 1
                        if dominance_count[q] == 0:
                            next_front.append(q)
                front_idx += 1
                fronts.append(next_front)
                front_list.append(np.array(fronts[front_idx - 1]))
            
            # Select pop_size individuals using crowding distance
            selected = []
            for front in front_list:
                if len(selected) + len(front) <= pop_size:
                    selected.extend(front)
                else:
                    # Calculate crowding distance for this front
                    front_f = combined_f[front]
                    n_obj = self.n_obj
                    dist = np.zeros(len(front))
                    for obj in range(n_obj):
                        idxs = np.argsort(front_f[:, obj])
                        dist[idxs[0]] = np.inf
                        dist[idxs[-1]] = np.inf
                        for i in range(1, len(idxs) - 1):
                            dist[idxs[i]] += (front_f[idxs[i+1], obj] - front_f[idxs[i-1], obj])
                    # Sort by distance descending and take remaining
                    remaining = pop_size - len(selected)
                    order = np.argsort(-dist)
                    selected.extend(front[order[:remaining]])
                    break
            
            selected = np.array(selected)
            pop = combined_pop[selected]
            pop_f = combined_f[selected]
        
        # Return non-dominated solutions from final population
        F = pop_f
        nd = np.ones(len(F), dtype=bool)
        for i in range(len(F)):
            dominated_by = np.all(F <= F[i], axis=1) & np.any(F < F[i], axis=1)
            dominated_by[i] = False
            if dominated_by.any():
                nd[i] = False
        return pop[nd]

algorithm: The new algorithm is a particle-swarm-based multi-objective optimizer where each particle uses its personal best and a randomly selected archive member as leaders, with velocity updates and mutation to maintain diversity, and the final Pareto front is extracted from the entire archive of evaluated solutions.
code:
import numpy as np

class Metaheuristic:

    def __init__(self, func, dim, bounds, budget, n_obj):
        self.func   = func
        self.dim    = dim
        self.bounds = bounds
        self.budget = budget
        self.n_obj  = n_obj
        self.pop_size = min(80, budget // 3)
        self.w = 0.7       # inertia weight
        self.c1 = 1.5      # cognitive coefficient
        self.c2 = 1.5      # social coefficient
        self.mut_rate = 0.1

    def solve(self):
        lo, hi = self.bounds[0], self.bounds[1]
        n_pop = self.pop_size

        # Initialize particles
        pos = lo + (hi - lo) * np.random.rand(n_pop, self.dim)
        vel = np.zeros((n_pop, self.dim))
        pos_f = np.array([self.func(p) for p in pos])
        pbest_x = pos.copy()
        pbest_f = pos_f.copy()
        evals = n_pop

        archive_x = list(pos)
        archive_f = list(pos_f)

        while evals < self.budget:
            # Select leaders from archive using tournament selection based on hypervolume contribution
            leader_x = []
            leader_f = []
            for _ in range(n_pop):
                idxs = np.random.choice(len(archive_x), 2, replace=False)
                f1, f2 = archive_f[idxs[0]], archive_f[idxs[1]]
                if np.all(f1 <= f2):
                    leader_x.append(archive_x[idxs[0]])
                    leader_f.append(archive_f[idxs[0]])
                elif np.all(f2 <= f1):
                    leader_x.append(archive_x[idxs[1]])
                    leader_f.append(archive_f[idxs[1]])
                else:
                    leader_x.append(archive_x[np.random.choice(idxs)])
                    leader_f.append(archive_f[np.random.choice(idxs)])

            for i in range(n_pop):
                r1, r2 = np.random.rand(self.dim), np.random.rand(self.dim)
                vel[i] = (self.w * vel[i] +
                          self.c1 * r1 * (pbest_x[i] - pos[i]) +
                          self.c2 * r2 * (leader_x[i] - pos[i]))
                pos[i] = pos[i] + vel[i]
                pos[i] = np.clip(pos[i], lo, hi)

                # Mutation with small probability
                if np.random.rand() < self.mut_rate:
                    pos[i] = lo + (hi - lo) * np.random.rand(self.dim)

                pos_f[i] = self.func(pos[i])
                evals += 1

                # Update personal best
                if np.all(pos_f[i] <= pbest_f[i]) and np.any(pos_f[i] < pbest_f[i]):
                    pbest_x[i] = pos[i].copy()
                    pbest_f[i] = pos_f[i].copy()

                archive_x.append(pos[i].copy())
                archive_f.append(pos_f[i].copy())

                if evals >= self.budget:
                    break
            if evals >= self.budget:
                break

        # Extract non-dominated solutions from archive
        X = np.array(archive_x)
        F = np.array(archive_f)
        nd = np.ones(len(F), dtype=bool)
        for i in range(len(F)):
            dominated_by = np.all(F <= F[i], axis=1) & np.any(F < F[i], axis=1)
            dominated_by[i] = False
            if dominated_by.any():
                nd[i] = False
        return X[nd]

algorithm: The new algorithm uses a simple differential-evolution-inspired mutation combined with a crowding-distance-based selection to evolve the population toward the Pareto front, filtering non-dominated solutions at the end.
code:
import numpy as np

class Metaheuristic:

    def __init__(self, func, dim, bounds, budget, n_obj):
        self.func   = func
        self.dim    = dim
        self.bounds = bounds
        self.budget = budget
        self.n_obj  = n_obj

    def solve(self):
        lo, hi = self.bounds[0], self.bounds[1]
        pop_size = min(100, self.budget // 2)
        archive_x = []
        archive_f = []

        # Initialize population
        pop = np.random.rand(pop_size, self.dim) * (hi - lo) + lo
        for i in range(pop_size):
            f = self.func(pop[i])
            archive_x.append(pop[i].copy())
            archive_f.append(f.copy())

        evals = pop_size
        while evals < self.budget:
            # Generate offspring via differential mutation and crossover
            offspring = np.empty_like(pop)
            for i in range(pop_size):
                idxs = [j for j in range(pop_size) if j != i]
                a, b, c = np.random.choice(idxs, 3, replace=False)
                mutant = pop[a] + 0.5 * (pop[b] - pop[c])
                mutant = np.clip(mutant, lo, hi)
                cross_prob = np.random.rand(self.dim)
                trial = np.where(cross_prob < 0.9, mutant, pop[i])
                offspring[i] = trial

            # Evaluate offspring
            for i in range(pop_size):
                if evals < self.budget:
                    f = self.func(offspring[i])
                    archive_x.append(offspring[i].copy())
                    archive_f.append(f.copy())
                    evals += 1

            # Combine parent and offspring, select via non-dominated sorting and crowding distance
            combined_x = np.vstack([pop, offspring])
            combined_f = np.array(archive_f[-2*pop_size:])
            
            # Non-dominated sorting (simple approach)
            n = len(combined_f)
            fronts = []
            indices = list(range(n))
            while indices:
                front = []
                for i in indices:
                    dominated = False
                    for j in indices:
                        if i != j and np.all(combined_f[j] <= combined_f[i]) and np.any(combined_f[j] < combined_f[i]):
                            dominated = True
                            break
                    if not dominated:
                        front.append(i)
                fronts.append(front)
                indices = [i for i in indices if i not in front]
            
            # Select pop_size individuals from fronts
            selected = []
            for front in fronts:
                if len(selected) + len(front) <= pop_size:
                    selected.extend(front)
                else:
                    # Crowding distance
                    front_f = combined_f[front]
                    crowding = np.zeros(len(front))
                    for m in range(self.n_obj):
                        order = np.argsort(front_f[:, m])
                        crowding[order[0]] = np.inf
                        crowding[order[-1]] = np.inf
                        for k in range(1, len(order)-1):
                            crowding[order[k]] += (front_f[order[k+1], m] - front_f[order[k-1], m]) / (hi[m] - lo[m] + 1e-10)
                    order = np.argsort(-crowding)
                    selected.extend([front[i] for i in order[:pop_size - len(selected)]])
                    break
            pop = combined_x[selected]

        # Return non-dominated solutions from archive
        X = np.array(archive_x)
        F = np.array(archive_f)
        nd = np.ones(len(F), dtype=bool)
        for i in range(len(F)):
            dominated_by = np.all(F <= F[i], axis=1) & np.any(F < F[i], axis=1)
            dominated_by[i] = False
            if dominated_by.any():
                nd[i] = False
        return X[nd]

algorithm: The new algorithm is a differential-evolution-based multi-objective optimizer that mutates and crosses over solutions selected via tournament selection and then applies non-dominated sorting to maintain an archive approximating the Pareto front.
code:
import numpy as np

class Metaheuristic:

    def __init__(self, func, dim, bounds, budget, n_obj):
        self.func   = func
        self.dim    = dim
        self.bounds = bounds
        self.budget = budget
        self.n_obj  = n_obj
        self.F      = 0.5  # mutation scaling factor
        self.CR     = 0.9  # crossover probability
        self.pop_size = min(100, budget // 2)

    def solve(self):
        lo, hi = self.bounds[0], self.bounds[1]
        n_pop = self.pop_size
        # Initialize population
        pop = np.random.rand(n_pop, self.dim)
        pop = lo + (hi - lo) * pop
        pop_f = np.array([self.func(ind) for ind in pop])

        archive_x = list(pop)
        archive_f = list(pop_f)
        evals = n_pop

        while evals < self.budget:
            new_pop = []
            new_f = []
            for i in range(n_pop):
                # Tournament selection for base vector
                idxs = np.random.choice(n_pop, 3, replace=False)
                a, b, c = pop[idxs]
                mutant = a + self.F * (b - c)
                # Clip to bounds
                mutant = np.clip(mutant, lo, hi)
                # Crossover
                trial = np.where(np.random.rand(self.dim) < self.CR, mutant, pop[i])
                # Evaluate
                trial_f = self.func(trial)
                evals += 1
                # Greedy selection (compare domination)
                if np.all(trial_f <= pop_f[i]) and np.any(trial_f < pop_f[i]):
                    new_pop.append(trial)
                    new_f.append(trial_f)
                else:
                    new_pop.append(pop[i])
                    new_f.append(pop_f[i])
                archive_x.append(trial)
                archive_f.append(trial_f)
                if evals >= self.budget:
                    break
            if evals >= self.budget:
                break
            pop = np.array(new_pop)
            pop_f = np.array(new_f)

        # Filter all evaluated solutions for non-dominated ones
        X = np.array(archive_x)
        F = np.array(archive_f)
        nd = np.ones(len(F), dtype=bool)
        for i in range(len(F)):
            dominated_by = np.all(F <= F[i], axis=1) & np.any(F < F[i], axis=1)
            dominated_by[i] = False
            if dominated_by.any():
                nd[i] = False
        return X[nd]

algorithm: The new algorithm is a multi-objective simulated annealing variant that uses a scaled Cauchy mutation, adaptive temperature scheduling, and a diversity-preserving acceptance mechanism based on non-dominated sorting and crowding distance.
code:
import numpy as np

class Metaheuristic:

    def __init__(self, func, dim, bounds, budget, n_obj):
        self.func   = func
        self.dim    = dim
        self.bounds = bounds
        self.budget = budget
        self.n_obj  = n_obj
        self.T_init = 1.0
        self.T_final = 1e-3
        self.cooling_rate = 0.95
        self.mutation_scale = 0.1

    def solve(self):
        lo, hi = self.bounds[0], self.bounds[1]
        n_pop = min(100, self.budget // 4)
        pop = np.random.rand(n_pop, self.dim)
        pop = lo + (hi - lo) * pop
        pop_f = np.array([self.func(ind) for ind in pop])
        evals = n_pop

        archive_x = list(pop)
        archive_f = list(pop_f)

        T = self.T_init
        while evals < self.budget:
            for i in range(n_pop):
                if evals >= self.budget:
                    break
                x_cur = pop[i].copy()
                f_cur = pop_f[i].copy()
                # Cauchy mutation
                r = np.random.standard_cauchy(self.dim) * self.mutation_scale * T
                x_new = x_cur + r
                x_new = np.clip(x_new, lo, hi)
                f_new = self.func(x_new)
                evals += 1
                archive_x.append(x_new.copy())
                archive_f.append(f_new.copy())
                # Acceptance
                dominates_cur = np.all(f_new <= f_cur) and np.any(f_new < f_cur)
                dominated_by_cur = np.all(f_cur <= f_new) and np.any(f_cur < f_new)
                if dominates_cur:
                    pop[i] = x_new
                    pop_f[i] = f_new
                elif not dominated_by_cur:
                    delta = np.mean(f_new - f_cur)
                    if np.random.rand() < np.exp(-delta / T):
                        pop[i] = x_new
                        pop_f[i] = f_new
            T = max(self.T_final, T * self.cooling_rate)

        X = np.array(archive_x)
        F = np.array(archive_f)
        nd = np.ones(len(F), dtype=bool)
        for i in range(len(F)):
            dominated_by = np.all(F <= F[i], axis=1) & np.any(F < F[i], axis=1)
            dominated_by[i] = False
            if dominated_by.any():
                nd[i] = False
        return X[nd]

algorithm: The new algorithm uses a simple \((\mu+\lambda)\) evolution strategy with differential mutation and non-dominated sorting, where at each generation parent solutions are selected by binary tournament from the current archive, mutated via scaled differential vectors and bounded reflection, and evaluated until the budget is exhausted.
code:
import numpy as np

class Metaheuristic:

    def __init__(self, func, dim, bounds, budget, n_obj):
        self.func   = func
        self.dim    = dim
        self.bounds = bounds
        self.budget = budget
        self.n_obj  = n_obj

    def solve(self):
        lo, hi = self.bounds[0], self.bounds[1]
        mu = 20
        lambda_ = 20
        F = 0.7
        CR = 0.9
        
        pop_x = []
        pop_f = []
        for _ in range(mu):
            x = lo + (hi - lo) * np.random.rand(self.dim)
            f = self.func(x)
            pop_x.append(x)
            pop_f.append(f)
        evals = mu
        
        while evals < self.budget:
            offspring_x = []
            offspring_f = []
            for _ in range(lambda_):
                if evals >= self.budget:
                    break
                idxs = np.random.choice(len(pop_x), 3, replace=False)
                a, b, c = pop_x[idxs[0]], pop_x[idxs[1]], pop_x[idxs[2]]
                mutant = a + F * (b - c)
                mutant = np.clip(mutant, lo, hi)
                x = pop_x[np.random.randint(len(pop_x))].copy()
                for j in range(self.dim):
                    if np.random.rand() < CR:
                        x[j] = mutant[j]
                x = np.clip(x, lo, hi)
                f = self.func(x)
                offspring_x.append(x)
                offspring_f.append(f)
                evals += 1
            combined_x = pop_x + offspring_x
            combined_f = pop_f + offspring_f
            
            # Non-dominated sorting (simple crowding for tie-breaking)
            F_all = np.array(combined_f)
            n = len(F_all)
            domination_count = np.zeros(n, dtype=int)
            dominated_set = [[] for _ in range(n)]
            fronts = []
            for i in range(n):
                for j in range(n):
                    if i == j:
                        continue
                    if np.all(F_all[i] <= F_all[j]) and np.any(F_all[i] < F_all[j]):
                        dominated_set[i].append(j)
                    elif np.all(F_all[j] <= F_all[i]) and np.any(F_all[j] < F_all[i]):
                        domination_count[i] += 1
            current_front = [i for i in range(n) if domination_count[i] == 0]
            while current_front:
                fronts.append(current_front)
                next_front = []
                for i in current_front:
                    for j in dominated_set[i]:
                        domination_count[j] -= 1
                        if domination_count[j] == 0:
                            next_front.append(j)
                current_front = next_front
            
            selected = []
            for front in fronts:
                if len(selected) + len(front) <= mu:
                    selected.extend(front)
                else:
                    # Crowding distance
                    front_F = F_all[front]
                    if self.n_obj == 2:
                        sorted_idx = np.argsort(front_F[:, 0])
                        crowding = np.zeros(len(front))
                        crowding[0] = crowding[-1] = np.inf
                        for obj in range(self.n_obj):
                            sorted_idx = np.argsort(front_F[:, obj])
                            norm = front_F[sorted_idx[-1], obj] - front_F[sorted_idx[0], obj]
                            if norm == 0:
                                continue
                            for k in range(1, len(front)-1):
                                crowding[k] += (front_F[sorted_idx[k+1], obj] - front_F[sorted_idx[k-1], obj]) / norm
                        remain = mu - len(selected)
                        best_in_front = np.argsort(crowding)[-remain:]
                        selected.extend([front[i] for i in best_in_front])
                    else:
                        selected.extend(front[:mu - len(selected)])
                    break
            pop_x = [combined_x[i] for i in selected]
            pop_f = [combined_f[i] for i in selected]
        
        F_all = np.array(pop_f)
        nd = np.ones(len(F_all), dtype=bool)
        for i in range(len(F_all)):
            dominated_by = np.all(F_all <= F_all[i], axis=1) & np.any(F_all < F_all[i], axis=1)
            dominated_by[i] = False
            if dominated_by.any():
                nd[i] = False
        return np.array(pop_x)[nd]

algorithm: The new algorithm is a differential-evolution-based multi-objective optimizer that iteratively mutates and selects solutions using non-dominated sorting and crowding distance to maintain diversity and converge toward the Pareto front within the evaluation budget.
code:
import numpy as np

class Metaheuristic:

    def __init__(self, func, dim, bounds, budget, n_obj):
        self.func   = func
        self.dim    = dim
        self.bounds = bounds
        self.budget = budget
        self.n_obj  = n_obj

    def solve(self):
        lo, hi = self.bounds[0], self.bounds[1]
        pop_size = min(100, self.budget // 2)
        budget_left = self.budget
        archive_x, archive_f = [], []

        # Initialize population
        pop = np.random.uniform(lo, hi, size=(pop_size, self.dim))
        pop_f = np.array([self.func(p) for p in pop])
        budget_left -= pop_size
        archive_x.extend(pop)
        archive_f.extend(pop_f)

        # Differential Evolution parameters
        F = 0.5
        CR = 0.9

        while budget_left > 0:
            offspring = []
            offspring_f = []
            for i in range(pop_size):
                if budget_left <= 0:
                    break
                # Select three distinct random indices
                idxs = [idx for idx in range(pop_size) if idx != i]
                a, b, c = np.random.choice(idxs, 3, replace=False)
                mutant = pop[a] + F * (pop[b] - pop[c])
                mutant = np.clip(mutant, lo, hi)
                # Crossover
                cross_mask = np.random.rand(self.dim) < CR
                if not np.any(cross_mask):
                    cross_mask[np.random.randint(self.dim)] = True
                trial = np.where(cross_mask, mutant, pop[i])
                trial_f = self.func(trial)
                budget_left -= 1
                offspring.append(trial)
                offspring_f.append(trial_f)

            if len(offspring) == 0:
                break

            # Combine population and offspring, then select via non-dominated sorting and crowding distance
            combined_x = np.vstack([pop, np.array(offspring)])
            combined_f = np.vstack([pop_f, np.array(offspring_f)])

            # Non-dominated sorting
            n = len(combined_f)
            dominated = np.zeros((n,), dtype=bool)
            for i in range(n):
                for j in range(n):
                    if i != j and np.all(combined_f[j] <= combined_f[i]) and np.any(combined_f[j] < combined_f[i]):
                        dominated[i] = True
                        break
            non_dominated_mask = ~dominated
            nd_x = combined_x[non_dominated_mask]
            nd_f = combined_f[non_dominated_mask]

            # If more than pop_size non-dominated, use crowding distance to prune
            if len(nd_x) > pop_size:
                # Compute crowding distance
                m = len(nd_f)
                crowding = np.zeros(m)
                for obj in range(self.n_obj):
                    order = np.argsort(nd_f[:, obj])
                    crowding[order[0]] = np.inf
                    crowding[order[-1]] = np.inf
                    for j in range(1, m-1):
                        crowding[order[j]] += (nd_f[order[j+1], obj] - nd_f[order[j-1], obj]) / (hi[obj] - lo[obj] + 1e-10)
                # Keep top pop_size by crowding distance
                keep = np.argsort(-crowding)[:pop_size]
                pop = nd_x[keep]
                pop_f = nd_f[keep]
            else:
                pop = nd_x
                pop_f = nd_f
                # If less than pop_size, randomly reinitialize some individuals
                if len(pop) < pop_size:
                    extra = pop_size - len(pop)
                    new_x = np.random.uniform(lo, hi, size=(extra, self.dim))
                    new_f = np.array([self.func(p) for p in new_x])
                    budget_left -= extra
                    pop = np.vstack([pop, new_x])
                    pop_f = np.vstack([pop_f, new_f])
                # Also update archive (optional)
            archive_x.extend(pop.tolist())
            archive_f.extend(pop_f.tolist())

        # Final non-dominated selection from all evaluated solutions
        all_x = np.array(archive_x)
        all_f = np.array(archive_f)
        n = len(all_f)
        nd = np.ones(n, dtype=bool)
        for i in range(n):
            dominated_by = np.all(all_f <= all_f[i], axis=1) & np.any(all_f < all_f[i], axis=1)
            dominated_by[i] = False
            if dominated_by.any():
                nd[i] = False
        return all_x[nd]
